figures.caption: Figure 1: Illustration of the reverse-time two-stage diffusion. Sampling starts from a noise prior and is guided by the learned score for τ > 0 to produce an in-distribution sample. At τ = 0, a physics guidance term, ∇Xτ log ps(Xτ |c), is switched on and guides the dynamics for τ < 0 to refine the sample toward the target condition outside the training distribution.

Figure 2: Airfoil dataset and mesh. (a) Representative Joukowsky airfoil geometries. “Geom-1” and “Geom-2” denote the two geometries analyzed in Sec. 4. (b) O-grid mesh around an airfoil. (c) Near-wall mesh detail.

Figure 3: Conditional score-model architecture. The noisy state, noise-level embedding, physical-condition embedding, and, for Q5, mesh embedding are combined in a U-Net backbone with residual blocks, skip connections, and attention modules.
Table 2: Score-model training and LAPG inference settings. The same conditional U-Net design is used for all cases, with case-dependent input size, resolution, and condition variables.

Figure 4: Trajectory generation in temporal and parameter extrapolation. Black: reference; green: data-driven diffusiononly; blue: training-time physics-constrained PINN; red: inference-time guided LAPG. Panels (a–c) Q1 (free fall); (d–f) Q2 (undamped spring–mass); (g–i) Q3 (damped spring–mass); (j–l) Q4 (point-vortex motion), with all trajectories plotted versus time t/τ . Gray shading denotes extrapolation (either beyond the training horizon or outside the training parameter range); panels with a white-to-gray split indicate the end of the training time window.

Figure 5: Potential flow past airfoils (Q5). Rows show one in-distribution target case (a–d) and two OOD target cases (e–l). The diffusion-only column (c,g,k) is generated at the nearest in-distribution condition c′ before action-guided refinement, while the other columns correspond to the target condition c. The colormap shows the streamwise velocity U/U∞, and the arrows indicate the freestream direction. Panels (a,e,i) reference; (b,f,j) LAPG; (c,g,k) diffusion-only; (d,h,l) PINN.
Figure 6: Quantitative error across all test cases listed in Table 1. Bars show the normalized root-mean-square error (nRMSE) for LAPG and the PINN baseline.
